title: Contaminant library: >= 8 core entries, plain-language, sourced to EPA/WHO, expert-reviewed
context: The contaminant library is the expert-reviewed reference layer that the engine uses to generate per-system explainers. Each entry must explain what the contaminant is, what the regulatory limits (MCL and MCLG) mean, what a violation of each type implies, and what the data cannot tell the reader. The nitrate entry is particularly sensitive because it carries acute infant risk — it must note this neutrally and route to authorities without providing individual advice. Every entry requires expert sign-off by an approver distinct from the author.
objective: Author >= 8 contaminant library entries (lead, copper, nitrate, arsenic, total coliform/E. coli, TTHM, HAA5, PFAS), each: (1) plain-language and accessible to a non-specialist reader; (2) sourced to EPA/WHO with retrieval dates and licenses; (3) carrying no safety verdict, grade, ranking, or product recommendation; (4) noting appropriate contaminant-specific limitations (e.g. nitrate: acute infant risk, route to authorities, no individual advice); (5) expert-signed-off by an approver distinct from the author, with a PR-tied review-log entry.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 entries present: lead, copper, nitrate, arsenic, total coliform/E. coli, TTHM, HAA5, PFAS
Every figure and claim sourced to EPA or WHO with retrieval dates and license; no verbatim copying of copyrighted text
Every entry carries no safety verdict, grade, ranking, or product recommendation
Nitrate entry notes acute infant risk neutrally and routes to authorities; provides no individual advice or lab interpretation
Every entry expert-signed-off by a qualified approver (drinking-water / environmental-health / public-health professional or toxicologist) who is distinct from the entry's author
A PR-tied, append-only review-log entry committed for each entry: PR number, commit SHA, content version, reviewer + approver identity + credential, sources checked, any source-divergence decision
output: Document: >= 8 contaminant library entries in content/contaminants/ with expert review-log entries committed for each.
